stories.title: Why US and Russia are backing Viktor Orbán in Hungary election
stories.summary: As voters head to polls, Washington support and alleged interference from Moscow raise questions about influence The official announcement that JD Vance was to visit, days before Hungarians cast their ballots in a hotly contested election, was greeted by Budapest with no less than four exclamation marks and three emojis. “!!Official!!” Viktor Orbán’s political director, Balázs Orbán, wrote on social media as he confirmed the news. The White House said Vance, along with his wife Usha, will land i

stories.title: ‘Feels like history is being made’: will young Hungarian voters oust Orbán?
stories.summary: The rightwing populist has been in power for 16 years but a new generation of voters are preparing to vote for his opponent, polls suggest Hungary elections: what is at stake and who is likely to win? As he rushed to finish off his cigarette before heading to class, Ákos, 20, confessed that he has more at stake than most as Hungarians prepare to head to the polls in the coming days. “If things remain the same, or get even worse, I can’t see a future here,” said the aspiring teacher. “There are m

stories.title: After 16 years in power, could Viktor Orban finally be unseated?
stories.summary: Hungary is going to the polls in nine days - after 16 years in power, can Viktor Orban be unseated?

stories.title: Gyönyörű időnk lesz, de aztán jön egy hidegfront
stories.summary: Napos, gomolyfelhős idő mellett napközben 15-23 fok várható.

stories.title: Tapasztó Orsi és Tapasztó Levente: Innentől kifelé van, az a sötétség már nem lehet
stories.summary: Az eredetileg kutatófizikus pár erdélyi magyarságáról is mesélt és arról, hogyan lehet egy célért úgy küzdeni, hogy elfogadjuk annak lehetőségét is, ha nem lesz eredménye.

stories.title: Magyarpéterfalván nem sokan hallottak Magyar Péterről, de Orbánt mindenki ismeri
stories.summary: Magyarpéterfalván jártunk, egy zárt, még működő magyar közösségben a dél-erdélyi szórványban, ahol a mindennapokat nem a politika, hanem a megmaradás, a munka és a gyerekek jövője határozza meg. A magyarországi kampány mégis elér ide is, nem viták és érvek formájában, hanem leegyszerűsített, gyakran torz üzenetekként, amelyek beszivárognak az emberek nappalijába a tévén keresztül. A helyiek többsége nem vesz részt a választásban, nem is kettős állampolgár, de van véleménye a történésekről. És mi

stories.title: Kétharmadtól patthelyzetig: mit hozhat a választás, ha feltételezzük, hogy megtartják
stories.summary: Húsz éve nem volt nyíltvégű választási kampány Magyarországon. Az Orbán-rezsim autokratikus politikai berendezkedéséből fakadó erőfölénye és az egyenlőtől fényévekre lévő választási környezet ellenére, van esély az ellenzéki Tisza győzelmére. A kérdés azonban nem egyszerűen az, ki nyer és ki veszít, hanem az, hogy a választási eredmény milyen körülmények között, milyen legitimitást adva, milyen parlamenti erőviszonyokat előállítva és milyen következményeket előrevetítve születik meg. Bár a forga

stories.title: Magyar Péter mindenkit óva intene attól, hogy Rogán vagy Kubatov helyett börtönbe menjen
stories.summary: „Pancserpuccs” – jellemezte a 444-nek adott interjújában az elmúlt napokban kiderült titkosszolgálati akciókat, amikkel szerinte a választás lehetőségét akarta kiiktatni a hatalom.

stories.title: Magyar nyelvű rádiót indított az ukrán külügy, közleményben ítélik el a magyar állami propagandát
stories.summary: A magyarul, angolul és ukránul hallható közlemény szerint Ukrajna nem akar az orosz világhoz tartozni és békés viszonyt akar Magyarországgal.

stories.title: Nem minden menő konyha egyforma – milyen gyakornoknak lenni egy csúcsétteremben?
stories.summary: A világ legjobb étterme, a Noma vezetője lemondott bántalmazási vádak miatt, melyeket főleg tanulószakácsok hoztak nyilvánosságra. De a fine dining világa nem egyenlő a konyhai abúzussal. Egy magyar szakács meséli el, milyen egy dán csúcskonyhán és fermentáló műhelyben gyakornoknak lenni.

stories.title: Török Gábor kielemezte Magyar Péter utolsó heti országjárásának helyszíneit
stories.summary: Keddtől szombatig naponta 4-7 településre megy el Magyar Péter, hogy a helyi jelölt mellett kampányoljon. A kampányzáró esemény szombaton 18 órakor Debrecenben lesz.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor összevissza beszél kedvencfagyi-ügyben
stories.summary: Most akkor puncs vagy feketeribizli?

stories.title: Több Mozart, kevesebb háború
stories.summary: A Concerto Budapest Mozart-napjának főpróbáin és mesterkurzusán jártunk, ahol Elisabeth Leonskajával és Keller Andrással beszélgettünk.

stories.title: Magyar Péter: Soha nem látott politikai és nemzetbiztonsági bűncselekménysorozatnak vagyunk a tanúi
stories.summary: A Tisza elnöke a 444 szombat reggeli cikkére reagált, amiben egy korábban a Tisza Párttal kapcsolatban álló üzletember megfigyelési ügyéről írtak. A művelet az MP nevet viselte.

stories.title: Kigyulladt a trafó egy hévízi szállodában
stories.summary: Kis időre kiürítették a hotelt, 290 embernek kellett elhagynia az épületet.

stories.title: XIV. Leó pápa vitte a keresztet a nagypénteki körmenet teljes útvonalán
stories.summary: Ezzel ő lett a második pápa II. János Pál pápa után, aki mind a 14 állomáson át hordozta a fakeresztet.

stories.title: 4-0-ra alázta a Szoboszlaival és Kerkezzel felálló Liverpoolt a Manchester City
stories.summary: A tavalyi döntős Manchester így elődöntős lett az FA-kupában.

stories.title: Robert Fico beszélt Orbán Viktorral, majd az orosz szankciók eltörlését követelte az EU-tól
stories.summary: A szlovák kormányfő szerint haladéktalanul újra kell kezdenie a párbeszédet Oroszországgal.

stories.title: Sokkal nagyobb kárt okoztak az iráni drónok a rijádi amerikai nagykövetségben, mint azt tudni lehetett
stories.summary: Bár a március eleji támadásról azt mondták, csak kisebb tüzet és károkat okozott, a WSJ értesülései szerint fél napon át tomboló tűz és három megrongálódott emelet lett az eredménye.

stories.title: Hatszázezer depressziós országa vagyunk, mégis akad kórház, ahol száz pszichiátriai ágyra jut egy orvos
stories.summary: Az ország első számú pszichiátriai központjának főigazgatója ugyan lemondott, de a szakemberek szerint ettől nem oldódik meg a pszichiátriai ellátás válsága. Hiába kért Orbán Viktor tanácsokat a WHO-tól, ha ezeket tíz év alatt se sikerült megvalósítani.

stories.title: Első magyarként nyert külföldön a dartsjátékos Kovács Patrik
stories.summary: A magyar dartsos 6-3-ra legyőzte az angol ellenfelét a PDC European Touron.

stories.title: Csak a szerb határig vitte a MÁV utasait a vonatpótló busz, onnan sétálniuk kellett
stories.summary: A MÁV szerint a vonatpótló buszok nem mehetnek át a határon.

stories.title: Cuba begins to free more than 2,000 prisoners as US eases fuel blockade
stories.summary: Havana makes a Holy Week ‘humanitarian’ gesture as Russian tanker is allowed to reach oil-starved island Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Cuban authorities have begun to free prisoners after announcing they would pardon 2,010 inmates, the second release in less than a month as the country faces heightened US pressure. More than 20 inmates emerged from La Lima penitentiary in east Havana on Friday, holding their release papers, crying and hugging relat

stories.title: Trump a magyar GDP majdnem hatszorosának megfelelő összeget költene az amerikai hadseregre 2027-ben
stories.summary: Annyival nőhet az amerikai hadsereg költségvetése, amennyivel a második világháború óta egyszer sem. Hadihajók gyártása és az Aranykupola rakétavédelmi rendszer kiépítése is ebből valósulna meg.

stories.title: Sok izlandi magyar nem kapta meg a szavazási levélcsomagot, de ez nem tántorította el őket
stories.summary: Összefogtak, és közösen megoldották, hogy mégis tudjanak szavazni.

stories.title: Statisztának kirendelt katonák, ellopott éjjellátók és a valódi hős – Pálinkás Szilveszter a Sárkányok Kabul felett című filmről
stories.summary: Katonai szakértőként és színészként segítette a kabuli mentőakcióról szóló film forgatását a Telexnek kitálaló százados. A leszerelési kérelme benyújtása után szó nélkül lecserélték, és még a bemutatóra se hívták meg. Harcképtelenné vált Gidránról és napi 10 ezer forintért statisztáló katonákról osztott meg részleteket.

stories.title: Új kutatás bizonyítja, hogy Magyarország gyengébben teljesített az elmúlt évtizedekben, mint amire képes lett volna
stories.summary: Nem a pénzügyi világválság, még csak nem is a Covid-járvány a leszakadás kulcsa, hanem a keleti nyitás, az orosz külgazdasági kapcsolatok erősítése az európaiak rovására.

stories.title: 444: Az Alkotmányvédelmi Hivatal hónapokig lehallgatott egy Tisza Pártot támogató üzletembert
stories.summary: Az erről szóló jelentésekben következetesen használták az MP rövidítést, de a hivatal utólag tagadta, hogy Magyar Péterről lenne szó.

stories.title: Hungary's government accused of spying on opposition
stories.summary: Prime Minister Viktor Orban's government used the country's intelligence service to spy on the opposition Tisza Party, says investigative platform Direkt36. What does this mean for the upcoming parliamentary election?

stories.title: News outlets falsely report Somaliland called for extradition of Ilhan Omar
stories.summary: Reports, based on X post from unofficial account, follow JD Vance’s accusations and threats of finding ‘legal remedies’ Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox Several news outlets have falsely reported that Somaliland’s government called for the extradition of Ilhan Omar , basing their stories on a post from an X account that does not represent the state despite its claims to the contrary. Fox News, the New York Post, Sinclair Broadcast Group’s the National

stories.title: US authorities arrest relatives of late Iranian military commander who were living in Los Angeles
stories.summary: State department said niece and grandniece of Qassem Soleimani, killed in 2020 US drone strike, celebrated attacks against US soldiers US federal agents have arrested the niece and grandniece of the late Iranian military commander Qassem Soleimani after the Trump administration’s top diplomat, Marco Rubio , revoked their lawful permanent resident status, officials said on Saturday. “Hamideh Soleimani Afshar and her daughter are now in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement,” or IC

stories.title: RFI Investigation: Ukrainian military in Libya and the strike on the Russian gas carrier Arctic Metagaz
stories.summary: French journalists report the presence of more than 200 Ukrainian military specialists in Libya and their alleged involvement in the attack on the Arctic Metagaz vessel using a maritime drone.

stories.title: Meloni in the Gulf: harmony on gas and defence with Qatar, the Emirates and Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The focus of the talks of Italian prime minister with Qatar, the Emirates and Saudi Arabia was the restoration of energy supplies and security in Hormuz. Italy offered technical support for affected infrastructure and defence against Iranian raids.

stories.title: Trump weighs more Cabinet changes after Bondi ouster
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed frustration and disappointment with Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer — and is pondering making additional changes to his Cabinet. “He’s very angry and he’s going to be moving people,” an administration official familiar with the dynamics told POLITICO. That official and three other people with knowledge of Trump’s thinking around his Cabinet were granted anonymity to discuss the unresolved personnel issues. The ad

stories.title: Cubans study oil tanker diplomacy for signs of progress in secret talks with US
stories.summary: Despite hostile rhetoric Trump let a Russian ship break his blockade – could it herald a Venezuela-style outcome? When a sanctioned Russian oil tanker, the Anatoly Kolodkin, docked at Cuba’s Matanzas oil terminal on Tuesday, unloading 700,000 barrels of crude , it was not immediately clear why the ship had been allowed to pass through Donald Trump’s oil blockade. In January, the US president had proclaimed on social media: “THERE WILL BE NO MORE OIL OR MONEY GOING TO CUBA – ZERO!” yet last week

stories.title: France: Thousands join Paris suburb mayor to protest racism
stories.summary: Left-wing Mayor Bally Bagayoko has been subjected to racist abuse since winning a clear majority in the first-round vote. Prosecutors are investigating comments made on national television.

stories.title: US job growth shatters expectations, in boost to Trump
stories.summary: U.S. job growth surged past expectations in March and the unemployment rate dropped, giving President Donald Trump some breathing room as the economy comes under increasing pressure over the war in Iran and rising oil prices. Companies added a net 178,000 jobs last month, the Labor Department reported Friday, a huge swing from February, when the economy actually shed jobs. But it was roughly in line with January — a sharp fluctuation that is making it hard to gauge the job market’s health. But t

stories.title: Trump and the myth of American oil independence
stories.summary: When President Donald Trump announced Wednesday that he’s winding down the U.S. war on Iran and washing his hands of the embattled Strait of Hormuz, he invoked what he likes to call America’s “energy dominance”: The United States has become the biggest oil producer in the world, and no longer needs to secure the Persian Gulf by force. “The United States imports almost no oil through the Hormuz Strait and won’t be taking any in the future,” he declared. “We don’t need it.” That’s news to the oil

stories.title: Italy’s Meloni travels to Middle East in bid to ensure access to oil and gas
stories.summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni is on a surprise tour of Arab Gulf states that aims to ensure Rome continues to have access to crucial energy sources in the midst of the war in Iran. Meloni kicked off her two-day trip on Friday afternoon, meeting with Saudi Arabian Crown Prince Mohammad bin Salman in Jeddah before traveling to Qatar and the United Arab Emirates on Saturday. Due to security concerns, the visit — the first by a European leader to the region since the start of the war — was k

stories.title: Egypt won’t accept Ukrainian wheat exported by Russia, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: Egypt will refuse to buy Russian shipments of grain sourced in occupied Ukraine, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Friday. “Spoke with Egyptian President Abdel Fattah al-Sisi, who told me Egypt will no longer accept grain exported by Russia from our temporarily occupied territories,” Zelenskyy wrote on Telegram , adding that Cairo had expressed an interest in increasing grain imports from Ukraine. The move marks a major shift in Egyptian policy. The country is

stories.title: ‘Racist ideology lodges itself amid austerity politics’: newly elected French mayor on tackling far right
stories.summary: Bally Bagayoko, who was targeted by racist abuse after winning Saint-Denis mayorship, vows to tackle inequality to stem deepening divisions A French mayor who faced a barrage of racist insults that are being investigated by police has called for France to urgently tackle race hatred and stem the rise of far-right ideas ahead of next year’s presidential election. “It has become a lot easier for racist views to be expressed … and unfortunately racist comments are becoming trivialised,” said Bally

stories.title: Biometric checks stalled again for cross-Channel travellers
stories.summary: Fears of Easter chaos over scaling up of new EU border system are eased, with no facial IDs for Eurotunnel and Eurostar passengers Passengers crossing the Channel from the UK to France will not face new biometric checks in the coming weeks, despite an imminent deadline for the complete implementation of the EU’s entry-exit system (EES), ports say. Airlines and airports across Europe have feared chaos over the Easter holidays. Continue reading...

stories.title: Unions privately voice misgivings over BMA pay demands and doctors’ strikes
stories.summary: Senior figures express concerns over medical union’s refusal of pay rise that is higher than offer to other NHS staff Trade unions have privately expressed qualms about the forthcoming doctors’ strikes, expressing frustration at the conduct of the talks and the demands of the British Medical Association. The BMA is pushing for a pay rise higher than the 3.5% offered to doctors by the government, with strikes planned for next week . Continue reading...

stories.title: Second suspect arrested in Brooklyn stray-bullet killing of seven-month-old baby
stories.summary: Matthew Rodriguez, 18, was apprehended in Pennsylvania in connection to shooting that killed Kaori Patterson-Moore A second suspect in the stray-bullet killing of a seven-month-old baby on a Brooklyn street was arrested on Friday, investigators said, two days after a shooting the New York police department (NYPD) commissioner called “a tragedy that truly shocks the conscience”. Matthew Rodriguez, 18, was apprehended in Pennsylvania by NYPD detectives working with US marshals, according to author

stories.title: Search for missing US crew member of downed fighter jet enters second day
stories.summary: Military pilots said F-15 crew member would be trying to hide for as long as possible from the Iranian military Middle East crisis – live updates US search and rescue efforts for the missing second crew member of the downed F-15E fighter jet continued into a second day as Iran came under heavy bombing and Israel extended the war in Lebanon. A pilot had been rescued on Friday after the F-15E Strike Eagle became the first US plane to be downed over Iran during the five-week-long war, but the secon

stories.title: Federal judge halts White House effort to collect university data on applicants’ race
stories.summary: Trump ordered data collection after raising concern about race being used as factor in college admissions A federal judge on Friday halted efforts by the Trump administration to collect data that proves higher education institutions aren’t considering race in admissions. The ruling from the US district court judge F Dennis Saylor IV in Boston granting the preliminary injunction follows a lawsuit filed earlier this month by a coalition of 17 Democratic state attorneys general. It will only apply

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: US and Iran race to find missing pilot; Trump warns Tehran over strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Iran’s Revolutionary Guard Corps said it was combing an area near where plane came down in south-western Iran ‘I’m regretting everything’: #MilitaryTok reactions to war stray from White House messaging Iran has executed two men convicted of membership in a banned opposition group and carrying out disruptive actions aimed at overthrowing the Islamic republic, the judiciary said . The executions on Saturday were the latest in a series targeting members of the banned People’s Mujahedin of Iran (MEK

stories.title: Confidential report found former home affairs boss Michael Pezzullo was ‘reckless’ in engagement with Liberal powerbroker
stories.summary: Previously unreleased report obtained via freedom of information battle says Pezzullo exceeded ‘boundaries of normal public service practice’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The former head of the Department of Home Affairs’ engagement with a Liberal powerbroker was “reckless”, “ill-advised” and beyond the boundaries of normal public service practice, a previously unreleased confidential report found. The independent probe led to the sacking of Michael Pezzullo as se

stories.title: German, EU finance ministers call for energy windfall tax
stories.summary: The war in Iran has sent oil and gas prices soaring, boosting profits for energy companies, but burdening consumers. The EU is looking for a way to respond.

stories.title: Kenya: Energy executives step down amid fuel manipulation probe
stories.summary: Five senior figures were arrested on allegations of purchasing an emergency fuel shipment at inflated prices. They are accused of trying to exploit the fuel crisis caused by the war in Iran.

stories.title: Fifteen dead in 'massive' Russian attack while Kyiv signals readiness for Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: Russian strikes killed 15 people in a "massive" assault on Ukraine on Friday and into Saturday while Kyiv proposed an Easter truce and launched retaliatory drone strikes deep into Russian territory.

stories.title: Senegal bans ministers from foreign travel as oil price rise bites
stories.summary: The fuel cost is nearly double what the government budgeted for putting pressure on stretched finances.

stories.title: US and Iran race to find missing downed pilot as Lebanon faces intense Israeli strikes
stories.summary: The Israeli military have relaunched evacuation orders from the southern Lebanese city of Tyre, warning of more strikes, claiming they are targeting Hezbollah. Over 1.2 million people in Lebanon have been displaced since the beginning of the war.

stories.title: US says it has arrested relatives of late Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
stories.summary: The niece and grand-niece of Qasem Soleimani are in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, officials said.

stories.title: Trump wants the world to buy more US oil. He might regret it.
stories.summary: President Donald Trump wants the world to buy more U.S. oil, but he might regret the offer if it raises gasoline prices. Trump in his speech Wednesday night specifically called on countries dealing with fuel supply shocks brought by the U.S. and Israeli war against Iran to purchase more from America: “Buy oil from the United States of America. We have plenty.” But while the United States does have a lot of oil, it does not have enough to make up for the supply bottled up behind the Strait of Hor

stories.title: NATO’s Rutte to meet with Trump at White House amid attacks on alliance
stories.summary: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte will meet with U.S. President Donald Trump next week, setting up a potentially heated dialogue after Trump’s frequent attacks on NATO allies’ lukewarm support for the war in Iran. Rutte will travel to Washington to meet with Trump on Wednesday, Rutte’s office announced in a social media post. He’ll also meet with Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth. The meeting will come days after Trump said the idea of pulling the U.S. out of NATO

stories.title: One pilot rescued after US fighter jet crash in Iran
stories.summary: The U.S. military has rescued one of the pilots whose F-15 fighter jet crashed in Iran on Friday, as the search continues for the second crew member. A defense official confirmed Iranian state media reports about the incident and the rescue efforts. This is the first downing of a jet inside Iran since the war started and a possible blow to the Trump administration’s plan to quickly wrap up the weeks-long conflict. Iranian state media said Friday that a pilot ejected from the jet, although U.S. o

stories.title: Architect of the Easter Rising, hanged as a traitor: for Roger Casement, a pardon still seems far away | Rory Carroll
stories.summary: Relations with Britain have improved again since Brexit, but battles over Irish history remain visible in Stormont’s endless feuding Rory Carroll is the Guardian’s Ireland correspondent and author of A Rebel and a Traitor: A Fugitive, the Manhunt and Birth of the IRA More than a century after he was marched to the gallows, there is still something radioactive about Roger Casement , a name that continues to emit a faint crackle in British-Irish relations. He was knighted in 1911 by King George V

stories.title: Italian council buys Mussolini’s villa to keep it away from ‘fascist nostalgics’
stories.summary: Riccione’s leftwing mayor, Daniela Angelini, says public purchase is victory for town and ‘act of love and vision’ An Italian council has bought a villa where Benito Mussolini spent his summer holidays, partly to avoid the property falling into the hands of “fascist nostalgics”. Daniela Angelini, the leftwing mayor of Riccione, a town close to Rimini along Italy’s Adriatic coast, said the acquisition of Villa Mussolini through an auction was “an act of love and vision” and that bringing it back

stories.title: Lebanese forced to bury their dead twice as war robs them of final goodbyes
stories.summary: As Israel expands its invasion of southern Lebanon, people are having to bury their dead in temporary graves In Lebanon, the dead are usually given one last glimpse of their home town before they are laid to rest. Hoisted high above the heads of the living, their casket is slowly marched through the streets where they grew up. It is the hands of their loved ones that guide them into their final resting place, already dug, and gently sprinkle dirt on their body. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Not quite Greggs’: TikTok creators put London’s ‘gentrified’ bakeries to the test
stories.summary: Viral reviews of artisan cafes across the capital are sparking a debate over cost, culture – and who gets a slice of the city The video that started it all was innocuous enough: a woman in her 20s posted on TikTok about how she spends a perfect weekend in north London. On her list were the bakeries Jolene and Gail’s, and the De Beauvoir Deli. The reaction, however, was anything but. Many locals commented that they had never heard of the businesses she mentioned. One north Londoner, Moses Combe,

stories.title: Keir Starmalade, anyone? Will marmalade really have to be rebranded in UK?
stories.summary: Britain is reportedly considering aligning with EU rules in what Daily Mail is calling the PM’s ‘breakfast reset’ The story is, in Fleet Street terminology, a marmalade dropper. The name marmalade is being dropped. But is it? Continue reading...

stories.title: Parts of UK braced for heavy snow and gale-force winds as Storm Dave arrives
stories.summary: Storm expected to cause Easter weekend travel disruption, though warm weather could return next week Storm Dave is expected to cause travel disruption this Easter weekend, with warnings for heavy snow and gale-force winds issued across northern parts of the UK, but a reprieve from the cold snap could be on the way, with temperatures forecast to reach the mid-20s next week. The Met Office has issued a yellow severe weather warning in Scotland for heavy snow and blizzards causing some travel and p

stories.title: What are peptides, are they safe and is there evidence to back up the hype?
stories.summary: Influencers and athletes are among those claiming substances can help with injury repair, weight loss and angi-ageing Medicines watchdog to investigate UK peptide clinics over health claims From influencers to athletes, high-profile figures are hailing peptides as the route to wellness, claiming they help with injury repair, weight loss, anti-ageing and mood. We take a look at what these substances are, and the murky industry surrounding them. Continue reading...

stories.title: Medicines watchdog to investigate UK peptide clinics over health claims
stories.summary: Exclusive: Guardian investigation finds several clinics making potentially unlawful claims about benefits of unregulated therapies What are peptides, are they safe and is there evidence to back up the hype? The medicines regulator is investigating whether UK clinics are breaking the law by making claims about the benefits of unregulated, experimental peptide therapies, the Guardian can reveal. Interest in experimental peptides has boomed in recent years. The substances are delivered by injection

stories.title: Fugitive mafia boss wanted for murder arrested at Amalfi coast luxury villa
stories.summary: Roberto Mazzarella, head of a notorious Camorra clan, had been on the run for more than a year An Italian mafia boss, who was one of Italy’s most dangerous fugitives, has been arrested on murder charges after more than a year on the run, Italian police said on Saturday. Roberto Mazzarella was the head of the notorious Mazzarella clan of the Camorra – the Naples-based organised crime gang. Continue reading...

stories.title: California protection crews contain parts of wildfire that burned 4,100 acres
stories.summary: Springs fire, which had spread quickly by windy conditions, at least 45% contained on Saturday, say fire officials California fire protection crews on Saturday were getting a handle on the wildfire that broke out the previous evening in Riverside county, fanned by high winds that quickly spread the flames to more than 4,100 acres. The Springs fire, about 64 miles (103km) east of Los Angeles, was at least 45% contained on Saturday, a fire department spokesperson said. It was 25% contained late on

stories.title: One of two US crew members rescued after F-15E jet shot down over Iran
stories.summary: Officials say other remains missing in first downing of US fighter plane since start of war Middle East crisis – live updates One US service member has been rescued after a US F-15E Strike Eagle fighter was shot down over Iran, prompting a frantic effort to locate its two-strong crew, in the first such incident since the war began almost five weeks ago. US officials familiar with the situation said one crew member was still missing late on Friday, after Iranian state media released images of a t

stories.title: ‘India is going to face a food crisis’: Farmers panic over fertiliser shortages amid Iran war
stories.summary: Ripple effects of oil and fertiliser shortage felt by farmers in India and Sri Lanka despite governments saying there is enough stock to go round Gurvinder Singh never thought the war in Iran would touch his quiet corner of Punjab. Yet looking out over his smallholding, where he alternates between wheat and rice crops in the state known as India’s breadbasket, the 52-year-old farmer can barely think of anything else. His anxiety over a conflict playing out thousands of miles away is crippling as

stories.title: Nine charged over alleged conspiracy to import tonnes of cocaine and meth via ‘mother ship’ in Australian waters
stories.summary: Police allege drugs were to be collected from a drop zone in Bass Strait and distributed across the nation using trucking connections Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast When a commercial trawler sank off Victoria with four crew members needing rescuing, police became suspicious about an alleged drug trafficking operation. Nine men are accused over a conspiracy to import tonnes of cocaine and methamphetamine before distributing the drugs across Australia using trucking c

stories.title: Ukraine: 14 people killed in 'massive' Russian attack on 1,500th day of war
stories.summary: Ukrainian authorities say Russia has deployed "new tactics" as the country came under rolling aerial daytime assaults from missiles and drones fitted with jet engines. At least 14 people have been killed across Ukraine.

stories.title: India news: LPG tanker headed for Mumbai crosses Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Green Sanvi becomes the seventh India-bound LPG vessel through the strait amid Iran war disruptions, as New Delhi scrambles to secure fuel supplies. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Artemis II passes halfway point to moon, sends Earth photos
stories.summary: Artemis II is now closer to the moon than the Earth. The astronauts on board have sent back eye-catching photos of our planet, and fixed a malfunctioning toilet along the way.

stories.title: Germany news: People change Easter holiday plans amid crises
stories.summary: Germans are altering their travel plans for Easter and the months ahead due to conflicts, a new survey shows. The economy minister has rejected tax rebates and speed limits to tackle high fuel prices. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Why have US-South Africa relations soured?
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump wants to sideline South Africa on the world stage. What is his issue with the country and when did things start going south?

stories.title: Young German men refusing military service
stories.summary: Military service remains voluntary in Germany. However, with the Bundeswehr needing more soldiers, conscription could soon return. But an increasing number of young men are choosing to opt out.

stories.title: German men need military permit for extended stays abroad
stories.summary: The new military service law requires all men under 45 to seek approval from the Bundeswehr to leave the country for longer than three months. It also obliges the military career center to issue it.

stories.title: Displaced in Lebanon: 'Lives turned upside down'
stories.summary: Over 1.1 million Lebanese have fled their homes due to fighting in the south of the country and Israeli bombing of Beirut's suburbs. DW met a family struggling to preserve a sense of normality in tough conditions.

stories.title: Trump seeks $1.5T for defense, 10% cut to domestic programs
stories.summary: The US budget proposals come as Washington faces rising costs from the Iran war and seeks to rebuild weapons stockpiles. The president said he also wants to cut back or eliminate "woke, weaponized and wasteful programs."

stories.title: Iran war: Search on for missing crew member of downed US jet
stories.summary: The US is racing against time and Iranian forces to recover a missing pilot of an F-15 fighter jet that was downed over Iran. Meanwhile, the UN Security Council has delayed a vote on the use of force to reopen Hormuz.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV bares cross for full Good Friday procession, a feat not seen in decades
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV marked his first Good Friday by becoming the first pontiff in decades to personally carry the wooden cross for all 14 stations at the Colosseum.

stories.title: Russia chose 'Easter escalation' over ceasefire, says Zelensky
stories.summary: Six civilians were killed and 40 others injured as Russia launched hundreds of drones and missiles.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV carries cross through all 14 stations at Rome’s Colosseum
stories.summary: Italy witnessed a rare and highly symbolic Good Friday ceremony on 3 April as Pope Leo XIV carried a wooden cross through all 14 stations of the Way of the Cross at Rome’s Colosseum, the first time in decades that a pope has performed the full rite in person.

stories.title: Trump seeks $152m to reopen notorious Alcatraz prison
stories.summary: Former Speaker of the House Nancy Pelosi calls the plan a "stupid notion that would be nothing more than a waste of taxpayer dollars".

stories.title: Middle East tensions drives global food prices higher for second month
stories.summary: The UN reported a second monthly rise in global food prices as the Middle East conflict drives up energy and fertilizer costs, threatening future harvests and yields despite currently stable market supplies.

stories.title: Rescue team in Iran face 'harrowing and dangerous' search for US crew member
stories.summary: A former US marine tells the BBC the priority of any recovery team would be to look for signs of life.

stories.title: What we know so far about the search for missing US airman in Iran
stories.summary: US and Iranian forces are searching for a missing American crew member after a US warplane was shot down.

stories.title: Fugitive mafia boss wanted for murder arrested in Amalfi Coast luxury villa
stories.summary: Roberto Mazzarella has been on the run for more than a year and was living under a false name, police say.

stories.title: Australians told to continue Easter travel plans despite fuel shortages
stories.summary: Hundreds of petrol stations across Australia have run out of fuel as the Iran war disrupts global oil shipments and pushes up prices.

stories.title: Russian attack on Ukraine market kills five
stories.summary: A Russian drone hit a busy spot in the southern Ukrainian town on Saturday morning, injuring another 21 people.

stories.title: New German conscription law: eligible men may now need approval for trips abroad
stories.summary: Men aged 17 to 45 may now need approval from the Bundeswehr for trips abroad lasting more than three months. What is behind the new military service law?

stories.title: UN watchdog voices 'deep concern' as Iran reports new attacks on nuclear plant
stories.summary: The International Atomic Energy Agency urges restraint to avoid a nuclear accident at Iran's Bushehr plant.

stories.title: Prayers, verses and holy books: How have religious symbols been used in the ongoing war in the Middle East?
stories.summary: The messages exchanged in the Middle East reflect a different face of warfare. Religious rhetoric is being employed in an uncommon and increasing way, with symbols of the three major monotheistic religions overlapping: Judaism, Christianity, and Islam in the middle of war.

stories.title: Cuba begins releasing more than 2,000 prisoners as US pressure mounts
stories.summary: US President Trump has made clear his desire to change Cuba's leadership and has placed the country under an oil embargo.

stories.title: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing appointed president after ‘sham’ election
stories.summary: Min Aung Hlaing seized control five years ago and plunged Myanmar into conflict and economic chaos Min Aung Hlaing, the military general who plunged Myanmar into conflict and economic chaos when he took power in the 2021 coup has been appointed president, months after widely condemned sham elections. Min Aung Hlaing, who is wanted by the prosecutor of the international criminal court for crimes against humanity against the Rohingya Muslim minority, was voted president by lawmakers on Friday. Mya

stories.title: 'This has got me worried': Iranians fear what comes next after US strike on Karaj bridge
stories.summary: President Trump has warned Iran of strikes on bridges and electric power plants if its leaders do not agree to his terms to end the war.

stories.title: Israel's PM says 70% of Iran's steel production capacity destroyed, hindering weapons production
stories.summary: Friday began with Israel saying it was under attack by a new barrage of missiles from Iran, as President Donald Trump warned the United States had yet to begin "destroying what's left" of the Islamic republic's infrastructure.

stories.title: ‘The frontline is like Terminator’: fighting robots give Ukraine hope in war with Russia
stories.summary: Use of unmanned ground vehicles has grown exponentially since 2024 turning the war into a technological contest Victor Pavlov showed off Ukraine’s newest and most versatile weapon: a battery-powered land robot. The unmanned ground vehicles come in various shapes and sizes. One runs on caterpillar tracks and resembles a roofless milk float. Another has wheels and antennas. A third carries anti-tank mines. Since spring 2024 their use has grown exponentially. Continue reading...

stories.title: Victoria police arrest two people as part of Dezi Freeman investigation
stories.summary: Man and woman released pending further enquiries after arrests at separate properties in state’s north-east on Saturday morning Two people have been arrested as part of the investigation into how Porepunkah fugitive Dezi Freeman was able to survive on the run for seven months before he was shot dead on Monday . A man and a woman were arrested at separate properties in north-east Victoria on Saturday morning around 7am, before being later released. Continue reading...

stories.title: Afghanistan earthquake of magnitude 5.9 kills at least 8
stories.summary: Authorities said that damage remains under assessment and may be delayed because of the remoteness of the center of the quake. Tremors were also felt in parts of Pakistan and India, including in Islamabad and New Delhi.

stories.title: How South Korea is using K-beauty trend to boost soft power
stories.summary: K-beauty is more than just a cosmetics trend. South Korea has combined popular culture, innovation and national strategy to create a business model where beauty products are an economic and political tool.

stories.title: 'Drill baby drill': Trump opens wilderness to big energy
stories.summary: America's beloved national parks and public lands face conservation rollbacks and sell-offs as the Trump administration pushes for fossil fuel and timber extraction.

stories.title: Unanswered questions remain after Australia's most wanted fugitive killed in standoff
stories.summary: Double-murderer Dezi Freeman evaded capture for seven months in the bush but police believe he had help.

stories.title: Faced with new energy shock, Europe asks if reviving nuclear is the answer
stories.summary: As war drives up gas and fuel prices, Europeans turn again to the issue of energy independence.

stories.title: How Bombay went from a fort city to a bustling metropolis
stories.summary: A new exhibition charts Bombay's passage through the centuries via paintings, photographs and prints.

stories.title: 'We want a voice in our land' - the people evicted to build Nigeria's capital
stories.summary: People evicted to build Nigeria's capital accuse the government of failing to fulfill its promises to them.

stories.title: How Canada's largest gun control effort in decades is missing the mark
stories.summary: Canada recently banned 2,500 models of "assault-style" firearms - but a plan to buy-back tens of thousands of guns fell short by half.

stories.title: Artemis II crew now halfway to Moon as they take 'spectacular' image of Earth
stories.summary: The snap was taken aboard the Orion capsule by its commander, Reid Wiseman, as the crew head towards the Moon.

stories.summary: A balesetben további két ember megsérült, a sofőrnél felmerült az ittasság gyanúja.

stories.summary: A lángok a 200 ezer lakosú Moreno Valley közelében csaptak fel, egy helyi főiskolát bezártak.

stories.summary: A 444 szombaton számolt be róla, hogy az Alkotmányvédelmi Hivatal hónapokig lehallgatta az üzletembert, a lehallgatás összefoglalóira „MP-s információk” néven hivatkoztak. stories.title: Székely alpesi életérzés: megcsúsztuk a Csicsói-Hargitát

Van itt minden, ami egy jó székelyföldi szociográfiához kell: retró felvonó, zúzmarától ufónak látszó fenyők, a hóba írt politikai véleménynyilvánítás és edzésben lévő hegyivadászok. stories.title: Political Capital: Árulkodó a Fidesz Mi Hazánkkal kapcsolatos B terve
stories.summary: Az intézet szerint az új terv nincs összhangban azzal a magabiztos arculattal, amit a kormánypárt közvetíteni kíván magáról.

stories.summary: A Telex élő közvetítése a közel-keleti konfliktus 36. napjáról.

stories.summary: Egy bikaviadalra készített fel a bikákat Málaga város egyik arénájánál, amikor az egyik állat rátámadt.

stories.summary: Az NVB szombati online ülésén többször felmerült az is, hogy a polgármesterek kampánycélokra használták a Facebook-oldalukat.

stories.summary: „Az igazság nem rémhír, forronganak az emberek, és szelep híján elviselhetetlenné nő belül a nyomás.” E heti novellánk egy ártatlan ember meghurcolásának története.

stories.summary: Intenzív osztályon kezelték, de nem tudták megmenteni az életét.

stories.summary: Az elmúlt öt évben a hálapénz eltűnt, de maradt a bizonytalanság és a szerencsefaktor a szüléseknél: sok kórházban automatikus a szülésgyorsítás és a gátmetszés, a császármetszések aránya pedig sosem látott, 42,55 százalékos magasságban jár. Cikksorozatban vizsgáljuk, hogyan hatottak a Fidesz-kormányok a nők életére, most a szülészeti ellátással foglalkozunk.

stories.summary: A Nagyon rossz előérzetem van című sorozatnál még egy sima esküvői készülődés is félelmetesebb. Pedig a színészek néha nagyon odateszik magukat, hogy a gyenge alapból is kihozzanak valamit.

stories.title: Alleged maple syrup scam in Quebec uncovered by Canadian broadcaster
stories.summary: A Radio-Canada reporter noticed his maple syrup tasted odd; testing revealed it was adulterated with cane sugar An investigation by Canada ’s national broadcaster has found that a major Quebec producer has been diluting its maple syrup with cane sugar and selling the fraudulent product to grocery chains. In a sting operation that involved false identities and covert recordings, journalists from Radio-Canada’s Enquête programme found that a low-cost syrup sold in major grocery store chains was he

stories.title: Canadian woman held with daughter by ICE is released after nearly three weeks of detention
stories.summary: Tania Warner is fitted with ankle monitor and released along with seven-year-old daughter Ayla Luca after being deemed not a flight risk A Canadian woman and her seven-year-old daughter, who were held for nearly three weeks in a notorious detention center by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), were released on Thursday evening after posting a bond of $9,500. Tania Warner and her daughter Ayla Luca, originally from British Columbia, are both Canadian citizens. Warner moved to the US in

stories.title: People of Burkina Faso should forget about democracy, says military ruler
stories.summary: Ibrahim Traoré, who took power in 2022 coup, tells state broadcaster ‘we must tell the truth, democracy isn’t for us’ People in Burkina Faso should forget about democracy as it is “not for us”, the military president, Ibrahim Traoré, told the country’s state broadcaster. Traoré took power in a coup in September 2022 , toppling another junta that had taken power just nine months earlier. He has since stifled opposition and in January banned political parties outright. Continue reading...

stories.title: What to know about Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche
stories.summary: The former prosecutor has a long-standing relationship with President Trump, having represented him in the criminal hush-money case.

stories.title: Watch: Strike hits major Iranian bridge near Tehran, killing at least 8 civilians
stories.summary: Iran said strikes on the unfinished B1 bridge in Alborz province killed at least eight people and wounded 95 on Thursday, after families gathered near the site for Nature Day celebrations.

stories.title: Tehran fires at Israel as Trump warns US has not started 'destroying what is left' in Iran
stories.summary: Israel's military reported a new missile salvo from Iran on Friday, with its air defences operating to down them, but there were no immediate reports of casualties.

stories.title: Kyiv region under 'massive' daytime drone and missile attack by Russia, officials say
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Kyiv was ready for a truce over the Easter holidays, but the Kremlin said it had not received any proposals.

stories.title: UK deploys Rapid Sentry anti-drone system to Kuwait to fend off attacks, air force says
stories.summary: Gulf countries including Kuwait, the United Arab Emirates and Bahrain have been pulled into the Iran war, targeted by Iranian strikes in retaliation to attacks launched by the United States and Israel on 28 February.

stories.title: Pete Hegseth asks US Army's top general to step down
stories.summary: Gen Randy George was nominated for the role in 2023 by former President Joe Biden.

stories.title: Italy's Uffizi Galleries targeted in cyber-attack but deny security breach
stories.summary: Hackers were reported to have infiltrated IT systems - but the gallery says its works are safe.

stories.title: US launches rescue operation after state TV says American fighter jet downed in Iran
stories.summary: The incident is the first report of a US fighter jet being downed in Iranian territory since the United States and Israel launched its war on the Islamic republic on 28 February.

stories.title: Trump seeks $1.5tn for defence alongside domestic spending cuts
stories.summary: The US president's new budget would cut non-defence spending by 10%, partly by slashing domestic programmes.

stories.title: Iran reeling after B1 bridge strike as Trump threatens further attacks
stories.summary: Images taken during a press tour of the site show collapsed sections of the roadway, twisted steel girders and extensive damage in the surrounding residential area where the B1 bridge near Karaj once stood.

stories.summary: Russian missile and drone strikes hit Ukraine’s Zhytomyr region, killing one and injuring 10, as dozens of homes and public buildings were destroyed.

stories.title: Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement
stories.summary: Dozen people arrive under new deal but legal challenges expected with scheme criticised as ‘dehumanising process’ A flight carrying people being deported from the US has landed in Uganda, as Donald Trump’s administration pushes on with its strategy of expelling migrants to countries they have no ties to. The deported people would stay in the east African country as “a transition phase for potential onward transmission to other countries”, an unnamed senior Ugandan government official told Reuter

stories.title: Trump undermining NATO by creating doubt about US commitment, Macron says
stories.summary: The US president has repeatedly railed against Western partners for not responding to his call to assemble a naval force to help reopen the crucial Strait of Hormuz, which has effectively been closed by Tehran.

stories.summary: From farms in New Zealand to factories in Delhi, the effects of the oil crisis triggered by the Iran war are rippling across Asia Continue reading...

stories.title: Stop brunch! How a rustic Catalan meal is taking the fight to bland food and overtourism | Abbas Asaria
stories.summary: As Barcelona groans under a surfeit of generic cafes, a grassroots movement is reviving the traditional ‘fork breakfast’. Anyone for pigs’ trotters? There are many worse ways to start your day than with eggs royale. The contrast in textures between a soft poached egg and a coarse, toasted English muffin is a thing of beauty, and the combination of smoked salmon and a lemony hollandaise sauce ties it together perfectly. The term “brunch” was coined in an essay in Hunter’s Weekly in 1895, and whil

stories.title: Storm Erminio lashes much of Greece as Saharan dust blankets Crete
stories.summary: South-east Italy also affected by heavy rain, and snow at higher altitudes, while deadly flooding hits Afghanistan Parts of the Mediterranean have been lashed by Storm Erminio this week. Heavy rain, thunderstorms and occasional bursts of hail affected much of Greece throughout Wednesday and Thursday, with the most severe conditions across south-eastern parts of the mainland and several islands in the southern Aegean Sea, including Crete, with streets flooded and vehicles stranded. stories.title: Germany's peace movement gears up for its traditional Easter peace marches, overshadowed by war and possible conscription
stories.summary: Germany's peace movement is gearing up for its traditional Easter peace marches, with the war in the Middle East and the possible reintroduction of conscription causing anxiety among Germans.

stories.title: Greek ministers resign over EU farming subsidy scandal
stories.summary: A scandal over the misuse of EU subsidies has triggered ministerial resignations in Greece. Beneficiaries allegedly made claims for land and livestock they did not own, waved through by lawmakers seeking votes.

stories.title: Iran war: One downed US fighter jet pilot rescued
stories.summary: One crew member of a US fighter jet downed over Iran has been rescued and an operation to find another is still underway, according to US and Israeli media. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Turkey and Armenia: When will the border crossings open?
stories.summary: Hostility between the two neighbors isolated them from each other for decades. Now Turkey's business community hopes the recent rapprochement will bring trade benefits. New logistics routes could boost the local economy.

stories.title: From the Chrism Mass to the washing of the feet: Pope Leo’s first Easter season begins
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV begins his first Easter as pontiff by washing priests’ feet, following a tradition previously used by Pope Francis, while warning against domination in a troubled world.

stories.title: Belarus passes bill to crack down on LGBTQ+ rights echoing similar law in Russia
stories.summary: Belarus decriminalised homosexuality in 1994 after the collapse of the Soviet Union, but it doesn’t recognise same-sex marriages and lacks protection for LGBTQ+ rights.

stories.title: Arlo Parks: 'I got out of my head and into my body'

stories.title: US Defence Secretary Hegseth asks US Army chief to step down as Iran war grinds on
stories.summary: Randy George 'will be retiring from his position as the 41st Chief of Staff of the Army effective immediately,' said Sean Parnell, the Pentagon’s top spokesman. George has held the post since August 2023 under the Biden administration.

stories.title: Researchers spent years interviewing 160 Bigfoot hunters - this is what they learnt
stories.summary: There have been thousands of reported Bigfoot sightings since it rose to prominence in the 1960s.

stories.title: Watch: Artemis II astronauts speak from space
stories.summary: The crew on board the Artemis II spoke by video link, sharing messages for their families and experiences of the trip so far.

stories.title: Belgium turns Easter chocolate into giant edible sculpture
stories.summary: Belgium is celebrating Easter with giant edible sculptures as nearly 40 chocolatiers from across the country present their creations in Brussels for the fourth Bel’Oeuf exhibition.

stories.title: Ukraine’s ancient Easter egg art helps preserve national identity
stories.summary: Ukraine is preparing for Easter with the pysanka, a hand‑decorated egg whose colours and symbols differ from one region to another and carry both religious and historical meaning.

stories.title: French ship crosses Strait of Hormuz in first Western European transit during Iran war
stories.summary: A vessel owned by France’s CMA CGM has become the first ship tied to Western Europe to cross the Strait of Hormuz since the outbreak of the Iran war in late February, according to ship tracking data.

stories.title: Three members of Greek government quit over EU farm subsidy scandal, state TV says
stories.summary: Most of the fraudulent subsidies went to the island of Crete, where the family of Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis has been politically influential for over a century.

stories.title: Burkina Faso must 'forget' about democracy, military leader says
stories.summary: Capt Ibrahim Traoré, who seized power in 2023, says democratic rule would not work for his country.

stories.title: Putin issues ultimatum to Armenia over closer ties with EU
stories.summary: In a rare blunt exchange at the Kremlin, Russia's president issued a public warning to Armenia, telling Yerevan to choose between the EU and Russia. The Armenian prime minister responded that in his country, the people of Armenia run a democratic political process.

stories.title: When will the Iran war end? Tracing the Trump administration's timelines
stories.summary: Experts say many US presidents have offered a timeline for a conflict - only to then shift their estimates.

stories.title: Ukrainian children taken at gunpoint by Russian forces in Kherson, documents show
stories.summary: Euronews obtained a copy of one of the documented episodes of Russia's systemic abduction and forceful deportation of Ukrainian children from temporarily occupied territories.

stories.title: Trump removes US Attorney General Pam Bondi
stories.summary: Bondi's time as top US law enforcement officer was overshadowed by her handling of the Epstein files.

stories.title: Who is Christopher LaNeve, set to lead the US Army?
stories.summary: He will replace Randy George, who was asked to step down from his post by Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth.

stories.title: France's Muslim gathering ban overturned by courts
stories.summary: The Paris police department had argued that the four-day gathering was a security threat because it could be a target of terrorism.

stories.title: Watch: Artemis II's journey so far as it leaves Earth orbit... in 85 seconds
stories.summary: The crew will not land on the Moon on this current mission, though Nasa is preparing for a potential lunar landing by 2028.

stories.title: Finnish conscripts train for all-out war with Russia
stories.summary: Finnish preparedness and deterrence against Russian aggression - whether in the form of hybrid attacks, or full-scale invasion, is long-established state practice. Conscripts are trained to be expert snipers; ready for all eventualities at the country's 1,350 km frontier with Russia.

stories.title: New Paris mayor vows to end all sexual violence in schools with €20m action plan
stories.summary: Claims of sexual abuse in schools were a central issue in the campaign leading up to last month's Paris mayoral election.

stories.title: US deports eight people 'of African origin' to Uganda
stories.summary: The Uganda Law Society criticises the deportation, saying it was illegal and "dehumanising".

stories.title: Rapper Gucci Mane kidnapped and robbed by fellow artist, prosecutors say
stories.summary: Officials say the hip-hop star was forced to sign papers releasing a rapper known as Pooh Shiesty from his record contract.

stories.title: Nigeria worshippers mark Easter with Lagos street procession
stories.summary: Nigeria marked Good Friday with hundreds of Catholic faithful marching through Lagos in a public reenactment of the Stations of the Cross, linking Christ’s suffering to the country’s economic pain.

stories.title: French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: It appears to be the first ship owned by a major European firm to go through the strait since the conflict began.

stories.title: Videos show explosions after a series of strikes hits Isfahan in central Iran
stories.summary: Massive explosions and thick plumes of smoke rose in Isfahan after a powerful wave of strikes hit central Iran on Wednesday. The attacks follow earlier US strikes in the same region that triggered a large fireball. Tehran retaliates with strikes on Gulf facilities and missile launches toward Israel.

stories.title: Albania on alert after US warning as Bulgaria confirms Iran diplomatic note
stories.summary: Albania is on alert after the United States Embassy in Tirana warned of possible threats linked to the Tehran regime. Meanwhile, Bulgaria confirmed it received a warning from Iran over US military use of its airports.

stories.title: Azerbaijan-European Union Relations: Current State and Prospects
stories.summary: Whether in politics, the economy or energy security, relations between Azerbaijan and the EU have been developing dynamically, writes Dr. Matin Mammadli in an opinion piece for Euronews. Increased investment, technology transfer and visa facilitation would further strengthen these ties.

stories.title: Israel’s military stretched thin by regional wars and settler violence
stories.summary: Amid reports of troop deployment to the West Bank to contain settler violence and warnings of manpower shortage by Israel's top general, Euronews spoke to two Israeli experts to gauge the pressure on the country's security and internal stability.

stories.title: French MEP Rima Hassan in French police custody over alleged apology for terrorism
stories.summary: Hassan, who was born stateless in a refugee camp for Palestinians in Syria, is one of France's most vocal pro-Palestine activists and has already had brushes with the law for perceived glorification of terrorism.

stories.title: New Zealand signs defence pact with Cook Islands after quarrel over China deal
stories.summary: Agreement comes after Wellington halted millions in aid to its former colony after Cook Islands formed strategic partnership with Beijing New Zealand and the Cook Islands have signed a defence and security declaration, ending a year-long diplomatic row that erupted after the Cook Islands struck strategic agreements with China. The Cook Islands was a dependent New Zealand colony from 1901-65 but has since operated as a self-governing nation in “free association” with New Zealand. Its roughly 17,0

stories.title: One killed and buildings damaged as magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia
stories.summary: Quake with epicentre west-north-west of Ternate island shakes cities and prompts regional tsunami warning One person has been killed after a 7.4 magnitude earthquake struck Indonesia’s Ternate island, damaging buildings and triggering small tsunami waves. The quake, which had a depth of 35km, occurred on Thursday at 6.48am local time, according to the United States Geological Survey. Its epicentre was 127km (79 miles) west-north-west of Ternate, an island in Indonesia’s North Maluku province. Co

stories.title: Crete skies turn eerie orange after Sahara dust storm – video
stories.summary: A Saharan dust storm blanketed the Mediterranean island of Crete as winds of up to force 9 on the Beaufort scale carried dust from north Africa, forcing several flights to be rerouted. The dust storm came as heavy rain and gale-force winds battered Greece, flooding dozens of homes and leading to at least one death Continue reading...

stories.title: Orange skies over Crete as Storm Erminio sweeps Saharan sand across Greek island – in pictures
stories.summary: Storm Erminio has caused widespread destruction in parts of Greece, including the Attica region where a man died in Nea Makri. On the island of Crete, skies turned an eerie orange as winds of up to force 9 on the Beaufort scale carried dust from North Africa, forcing several flights to be rerouted Continue reading...

stories.title: Trump’s surreal speech on Iran shed no light on his goals | Kenneth Roth
stories.summary: The US president couldn’t give a single coherent reason for why this aggressive war of choice must still be prosecuted Donald Trump’s self-congratulatory speech on Iran on Wednesday night was as puzzling as it was divorced from reality. I had hoped he would declare victory and end the war. Some feared he might provide cover for a ground invasion. Instead, he told us in essence to be patient, that he is almost done, but he was utterly unclear about what more there is to accomplish. If there was e

stories.title: Starmer calls for ‘ambitious’ new UK-EU ties as Trump threatens to quit Nato
stories.summary: PM to focus on European defence and economic partnership for ‘dangerous world’, in pivot away from US UK politics live – latest updates The UK will seek an even deeper partnership with the EU because of the instability wreaked by Donald Trump’s war with Iran, Keir Starmer has said, adding that the moment called for a more ambitious deal with Brussels to strengthen trade and defence. His comments came as the US president again said he was considering pulling the US out of Nato , which he describe

stories.title: UNESCO World Heritage sites facing the heat
stories.summary: From Easter Island to the Great Wall of China, many of the world's most iconic cultural heritage sites are under threat from rising temperatures linked to climate change.

stories.title: How US 'Liberation Day' tariffs reshaped global trade
stories.summary: One year after Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs, global trade flows have shifted. Trade data shows which countries gained, which lost — and who is footing the bill.

stories.title: Wealth tax: Why are countries afraid to tax the ultrarich?
stories.summary: Growing income inequality and public displays of billionaire power are increasing calls for new income and wealth taxes aimed at the world's richest individuals. Can wealth ever be taxed fairly?

stories.title: Semenya hits out at impact of Olympic ban on DSD athletes
stories.summary: Caster Semenya has had her gender scrutinized in public for years after winning two Olympic golds. She has hit out at what she sees as a policy that disproportionately affects athletes with sexual differences.

stories.title: As Iran war rages, Europe gears up for energy crisis
stories.summary: The EU is waking up to an energy crisis and asking people to reduce energy consumption, as fears of a supply crunch set in. Energy analysts are calling for concrete measures and a boost in investments for renewables.

stories.title: German Chancellor Merz has never been more unpopular
stories.summary: The CDU/CSU and SPD made many promises to voters. But according to polls, Germans are deeply disappointed with the governing coalition and have little hope that things will improve.

stories.title: Severe snow and floods in Italy leave residents trapped and damage infrastructure
stories.summary: Heavy rain and snowstorms in central and southern Italy brought down bridges and left people trapped in their homes on Thursday. Firefighters used special snowcat trucks to plow through huge banks of snow in small towns in the Italian region of Molise.

stories.title: Iran vows 'crushing, more destructive' attacks on US and Israel after Trump threats
stories.summary: The comments come after a prime-time address at the White House in which US President Donald Trump threatened to bomb Iran "back to the Stone Ages, where they belong."
